items.summary: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey tells broadcasters energy prices remain above pre-war levels and that the the central bank needs to assess "how much damage has been done to the infrastructure for supplying energy" in the Middle East. items.title: Ukraine drone strike hits Russian oil refinery , Zelenskyy says  Moscow will burn  if Putin continues war
US , Iran Sign Interim Deal to End Hostilities , Ease Sanctions ; Nuclear Talks to Continue
US Signs Deal With Iran, But Some Trump Allies Not Happy
items.summary: President Donald Trump signed an interim deal to end the war with Iran and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, but some Republicans say the US is giving up too much. Laura Davison reports on how the deal came together and what comes next.  (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: US-Iran deal: technical work can begin, says atomic energy agency
items.summary: The head of the UN’s atomic energy agency on Thursday welcomed the signing of an initial Iran-US memorandum aimed at ending the war, before proposing “to sit down” with both parties to assist with concrete measures such as verification of Iran’s nuclear programme, a key sticking point.

items.title: Wellington’s Geary Says Stocks Rotation Trade Is Coming Back
items.summary: The end of the Iran war is reviving investor appetite for stocks outside the US, according to Scott Geary, the head of global wealth at Wellington Management.

items.title:
China is deeply concerned over U.S.-Japan strengthening “extended deterrence”.

Japan portrays itself as a champion of a nuclear-free world, while relying more and more on U.S. “nuclear umbrella” and making no secret of its dangerous nuclear ambitions, posing a serious challenge to post-war order and the non-proliferation regime. The world must keep a close eye on whether Japan will honor its non-proliferation commitments and not seek nuclear weapons in any form.

Qatar Brings Empty LNG Ship Back for First Time Since War Began
items.summary: Qatar brought an empty liquefied natural gas tanker back into the Persian Gulf through the Strait of Hormuz for the first time since the war in Iran began, a sign that the key producer is preparing to ramp up exports.

items.title: SNB Keeps Up War Footing on Franc But Evolves Wording on Threat
items.summary: The Swiss National Bank warned investors that a Middle East peace deal hasn’t altered its state of readiness to sell the franc if such a stance is required.

items.title: South Africa Impeachment Panel to Oppose President’s Interdict
items.summary: A panel of South African lawmakers that is conducting an inquiry into whether President Cyril Ramaphosa should be impeached over his handling of a robbery at his game farm said it will oppose his application to halt their work.

Recommended: @ChannelNewsAsia documentary Inside Unit 731: Japan’s Secret Human Experiments 
https://youtu.be/AXM1DozZwjk?si=F_4Nn1EVquCUxKrm

During World War II, Japan’s Unit 731 conducted gruesome human experimentation and germ warfare during its war of aggression against China. 

This unspeakably dark and brutal chapter of history calls into question the very conscience and moral limits of humanity.

To this day, the Japanese right‑wing forces refuse to repent and have been trying to whitewash the notorious Unit 731 as a mere “sanitary research unit.” Attitude toward history is a mirror. It reflects the dangerous adventurism of Japan’s neo-militarism. The international community must stay on high alert.

items.title:
💬 #Zakharova: June 22 marks 85 years since the onset of the Great Patriotic War, a date forever etched in our national memory and history of humanity.

It lasted 1418 days and nights, claimed 27 mln Soviet lives, and left its mark on every single family across the nation.

items.summary: India’s central bank approved the re-appointment of Keki Mistry as part-time chairman of HDFC Bank Ltd., months after the abrupt resignation of his predecessor sparked questions about governance issues at India’s largest private-sector lender.

items.title:
With Prime Minister of Belgium @Bart_DeWever, we had a detailed discussion about support for our defense. Belgium is preparing a substantial new aid package and a contribution to the PURL initiative. We also went over the delivery schedule for F-16 fighter jets. The first aircraft will arrive this year. This is exactly the kind of support for protecting our skies that we need most right now. Thank you!

I briefed the Prime Minister on our meetings and agreements at the G7 Summit, as well as on our diplomatic engagements aimed at ending this Russian war. It is truly important that pressure on the aggressor continues to increase. We also discussed Ukraine’s European integration. I thank Belgium – the Government and all the people – for their support.

MFA spokesperson’s response to NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte’s accusation on China’s assistance to Russia:

NATO needs to address its wrong perception of China and stop inciting confrontation and shifting blame. As a Cold War relic, NATO needs to seriously reflect on what kind of effect it has had on peace in today’s world.

items.summary: At least four Saudi oil supertankers that had been idling in the Indian Ocean for weeks set sail toward the Gulf of Oman, the latest indication that producers are starting to gear up for a resumption of flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: North Korea warns of ‘disaster’ if G7 tries to strip its nuclear weapons
items.summary:
North Korea’s Kim ⁠Yo-jong, the powerful sister of leader Kim Jong-un, condemned a G7 call for the country’s denuclearisation as a violation of its constitution and an infringement of ‌sovereignty, state media KCNA said on Thursday.

items.title: Who pays for Iran’s recovery? US$300 billion fund emerges as flashpoint in ceasefire deal
items.summary:
A US$300 billion reconstruction fund for Iran has emerged as a flashpoint in a newly reached ceasefire agreement between Washington and Tehran, raising fresh questions over who will ultimately bear the cost.
Outlined in a 14-point memorandum of understanding (MOU) signed by the United States and Iran after more than 100 days of conflict, the fund commits Washington to work with regional partners o
